key_points: Er:YAG laser was used to activate 5.25% sodium hypochlorite irrigation during endodontic retreatment of post-treatment chronic apical periodontitis at tooth 47.
The laser-activated irrigation aimed to enhance root canal decontamination by exploiting photothermal effects on resistant microbial biofilms.
Cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) was used to assess treatment efficacy by comparing pre- and postoperative radiographic images.
At 3-month follow-up, radiographic assessment showed a tendency toward apical lesion regression and clinical improvement.
No postoperative adverse events were observed during the follow-up period.
Bacterial biofilm persistence in the endodontic system is identified as a key factor in post-treatment apical periodontitis.

outcomes.result: Tendency toward apical lesion regression confirmed at 3-month CBCT follow-up
outcomes.clinical_significance: Clinical improvement observed with no adverse events; single case report limits generalizability
Suggests acceptable safety profile in this single case

notes: Case report of a single patient (tooth 47). Er:YAG laser used specifically for activation of sodium hypochlorite irrigant (laser-activated irrigation/LAI), not for direct ablation. Specific laser parameters (wavelength confirmed as Er:YAG ~2940 nm, but power, fluence, pulse duration, frequency not reported in abstract).
source_citation: Zabrac, E., Chirila, M., Dragomirescu, A., & Suciu, I. (2026). Er:YAG Laser-Assisted Root Canal Decontamination in Post-treatment Endodontic Apical Periodontitis: A Case Report. Cureus. https://doi.org/10.7759/cureus.102709

treatment_protocol.application_technique: Laser-activated irrigation (LAI) with Er:YAG to activate 5.25% sodium hypochlorite

clinical_indication: Post-treatment chronic apical periodontitis requiring endodontic retreatment
uncertainties: Specific Er:YAG laser parameters (power, fluence, pulse duration, frequency, spot size, mode) not reported in the abstract or available full text.
Patient demographics (age, sex) not available from abstract.
Number of treatment sessions not specified.
Long-term follow-up beyond 3 months not reported.
Brand/model of Er:YAG laser device not mentioned.
Conflicts of interest and funding not declared in available text.
